description_long: This is a delicate, creamy-white flower with a prominent central structure featuring a cluster of red and white reproductive organs. The petals are broad, overlapping, and slightly cupped, forming a funnel-like shape that gently opens outward. Surrounding the flower are several large, ovate green leaves with smooth edges and a subtle sheen, attached to a slender, pale stem. The flower appears fresh and natural, with subtle veining visible on the petals and leaves. The overall form is soft and organic, with the flower head being the focal point and the leaves providing a lush backdrop. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A creamy-white, funnel-shaped flower with overlapping petals and a cluster of red and white reproductive organs at its center, surrounded by broad green leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open, creamy-white petals and the prominent red and white central reproductive organs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cupped shape of the petals and the cluster of green leaves and unopened buds behind the flower.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the overlapping petals from behind and the broad, ovate leaves extending outward.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the exterior of the petals and the attachment of the leaves and stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, overlapping whorl, forming a funnel-shaped corolla with a slight spiral tendency. The arrangement is symmetrical, with each petal partially covering the adjacent one, creating a layered effect.
